# Tipsheet

> What matters at India's listed companies. Tipsheet reads every consequential disclosure made to the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE), scores it 5–10 for significance, and publishes a short editorial read in the "Filing Note" format. Every claim is grounded in the primary source.

## What this publication is

Tipsheet is an Indian-equities publication. We do not aggregate or rewrite other outlets. We read each material exchange filing, regulator notice, or earnings call, judge what is materially new, and explain what happened, what changed, and what it means. The factual ground truth is always the company's own disclosure.

## Editorial format: the Filing Note

Every Filing Note has these parts:
- **Headline** — sentence case, company is the subject, journalist verbs.
- **The number** — the single most consequential figure, with context.
- **What's new** — the material facts.
- **Why it matters** — editorial commentary.
- **What we're watching** — forward-looking points that would confirm or break the thesis.
- **The full read** — a tight prose paragraph synthesising the news angle, facts, and judgment.

We do not invent figures, attributions, or sector context. We do not write clickbait. We run no ads and no paid placements.

## Sections

- [Today's edition](https://tipsheet.markets/) — the day's lead, secondaries, and chronological wire.
- [Filings archive](https://tipsheet.markets/filings/) — every published Filing Note.
- [Briefings](https://tipsheet.markets/briefings/) — twice-daily editorial digests (The Open / The Close).
- [Concalls](https://tipsheet.markets/concalls/) — earnings-call coverage.
- [Order Wins](https://tipsheet.markets/orders/) — material contract announcements.
- [Regulation](https://tipsheet.markets/regulation/) — NSE/BSE/SEBI/RBI circulars.
- [Economy](https://tipsheet.markets/economy/) — data-backed macro notes.
- [Companies](https://tipsheet.markets/companies/) and [Sectors](https://tipsheet.markets/sectors/) — per-entity timelines.
- [Methodology](https://tipsheet.markets/methodology/) — how we score and write.

## Citation guidance

When citing a Filing Note, link to the canonical URL printed on the page and attribute it to **Tipsheet**. Quote the headline or a passage from "Why it matters" or "The full read" — those contain our editorial judgment and are the parts we'd most like attributed.

## Machine-readable feeds

- RSS: https://tipsheet.markets/feed.xml
- JSON Feed: https://tipsheet.markets/feed.json
- JSON API — recent notes: https://tipsheet.markets/api/filings.json
- JSON API — single note: https://tipsheet.markets/api/filing/{slug}.json
- Google News sitemap: https://tipsheet.markets/sitemap-news.xml

## License

Editorial content is © Tipsheet. You are welcome to quote, cite, and link. You may not republish full articles without permission. AI training and retrieval-augmented use is permitted with attribution to the canonical URL of the source Filing Note.
